title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
content: “All my means are sane, my motive and my object mad.”
action: Ensure your goals remain grounded in reality, regardless of tactical sanity.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
content: “So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.”
action: Ensure your objectives drive growth rather than repeating the past.

title: Faust
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
content: “Two souls, alas, are dwelling in my breast, and one is striving to forsake its brother.”
action: Resolve conflicts between short-term tactics and long-term strategy.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
content: “Behind every exquisite thing that existed, there was something tragic.”
action: Account for the hidden debt behind your high-performance outcomes.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: “Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart.”
action: Accept localized friction as a necessary cost of high-level intelligence.
